reviews.review_text:
Leon is in a horror game.
Chris is in Call of Duty.
Jake is doing action movie stunts.
Somehow this is Resident Evil.

Explosions.
Helicopters.
Quick time events.
And occasionally...zombies.

Ps: My least favorite from 0 to Requiem.

Game is a 10/10 for me, 11/10 with a friend. It has many imperfections, but there's nothing out there that plays quite like it either.

Badly organized pro tips for having fun:

- Get in melee and use melee attacks. Shoot enemies in the head or knee to open up unique attacks; almost every enemy in the game has a melee prompt from multiple angles. Some bosses do too.
- Roll backwards, forwards, roll around on the ground. Roll sideways up the stairs and through vents. Aim + A button (X on PS controller) to roll around, keep holding aim to stay prone. Tap sideways to roll around.
- Hold B (or Circle on PS controller) and yell at your friends. Yell "NO" when they tell you to open a door. If you're at the door, spam the button until you start yelling.
- Left Trigger + Right Trigger together do a "quick shot" which takes pressure off and opens up enemies for melee follow ups. You get different animations for these ones VS the headshot stagger prompts. Also good for hitting small critters it's hard to aim at. Use these moves, explore your options.
- The healing item refills your stamina (see: melee meter). Using them next to a friend (both sprays and tictacs) heals you both for an empty square of health, plus whatever is currently partly-empty.
- Many attacks can be "parried" with the right timing of a melee prompt. More unique counters, animations, fun.
- I like all the campaigns, but I think the order from best to worst is Leon > Jake > Chris. I'd play Leon > Chris > Jake for a first go through.
- The inventory system sucks and gets worse when you have a full arsenal. Throw away ammo/grenades you don't like if you want room for other stuff, you'll replace it later. You can overflow your healing container up to 17 held at once (then it cannot hold more until you are at 11 charges or less).
- I've been playing it here and there for 13 years and bought it on 3 separate systems, but it goes on sale regularly for 5 bucks if you don't believe anything I'm saying and think it'll suck. Pretty low asking price for you and a pal to try it for a couple hours.
- Again, don't backpedal while shooting. That's boring and sucks. Jump backwards while blasting guns akimbo to avoid damage better and have fun. Run at enemies after you stagger them to suplex their heads into gelatin.
- Speaking of, many guns have alternate fire modes or ammo types. While aiming, hit Y/Triangle and it'll switch styles for you. Leon's pistol grows a second pistol.

Hope this inspires you to have fun!

The shift toward action didn't do this game any favors. It still feels just as clunky to control as the previous two entries, except now it's missing the things that made them interesting (at least in my opinion): inventory management, merchants, and the horror atmosphere

The co-op is decent (ALTHOUGH THERE ARE BUGS IN SHERRY BIRKIN'S CAMPAIGN!!!), with the exception of Ada's campaign. Your partner is basically a nobody who doesn't exist in cutscenes and can't even open a damn door

In short, every family has its black sheep. You can safely skip this crap

Resident Evil 6 is definitely more action-focused than classic Resident Evil, but I still had a lot of fun with it. Leon’s campaign was easily my favorite and felt the most like classic RE. Second to that would be the Chris campaign.
But, I wasn’t a big fan of the Ada and Jake campaigns though, especially Ada in co-op mode having some random soldier dude follow you with inconsistent cut scenes. It just didn’t feel as enjoyable as the others. Still, overall I think the game is worth playing if you enjoy action-heavy Resident Evil.

I honestly don't understand the hate on this game. Yes, maybe it crashes a lot, the controls are a little bad and confusing, but it's an old game. Despite that, this game is genuinely fun, especially when you're playing with friends. I like how CapCom switches between slow survival and full-on action games. If you do not want an action-based Resident Evil game, just don't play it.

this game is one of the most hated resident evil games, i can understand why people dislike it but its not all that bad. this game has over 20 hours of gameplay (not including dlcs) which imo is really good for its price.

the game constantly goes on a 75% discount every 1-2 months. and its one of my favorite multiplayer resident evil game. only complain i have is that the boss fights are not that fun. What I don't understand is why resident evil 5 gets so praised when this game almost twice as long and yet its almost just as fun.

Here's my ratings for all the campaigns:


[h3]Leon Kennedy's Campaign: ?/10[/h3]

Still haven't played played it so I will change this after I try it out.

[h3]Chris Redfield's Campaign: 9/10[/h3]

This was my favorite campaign, everything is perfect about it. I played as Chris and his assault rifle was really good. 

[h3]Jake Muller's Campaign: 7/10[/h3]

I'm currently in the middle of this one so the answer isn't complete but so far it has been really fun. Currently playing on professional difficulty

[h3]Ada Wong's Campaign: 2/10[/h3]

Ada's campaign was the one I started with, and that turned out to be a mistake. The campaign was very boring and the difficulty sucked. The weapons were disappointing too.

I played as Ada Wong, so I can't speak for Agent's weapon, but Ada's crossbow does little to no damage against the transforming enemies, which were everywhere. You're better off running past enemies rather than trying to shoot them.

I wouldn't recommend to start off with this one and its best to save it for last.

[h1] THE OVERHATED MASTERPIECE [/h1]


I’ve always been a huge fan of zombie apocalypse games - whether it’s [b]Dying Light[/b], [b]Left 4 Dead[/b], or even something a bit different like [b]The Last of Us[/b]. For a long time, I’ve been somewhat distant from the [b]Resident Evil[/b] series, despite knowing it forever. I watched many of its animated and live-action adaptations and always considered myself a passive fan.

I’ve played a few entries as well, including [b]RE2[/b] and [b]RE4[/b] - and the very first [b]Resident Evil[/b] game I ever played was [b]Resident Evil 6[/b]. Even though I absolutely love [b]RE4[/b] (and many would agree it’s a peak experience), I honestly don’t understand why [b]RE6[/b] gets so much hate. It has something unique to offer compared to the rest of the franchise.

I really enjoy co-op story-driven games, and [b]RE6[/b] stands out as one of the best examples of that style. The ability to play the entire campaign with a friend, and even briefly connect with other players when different character storylines cross paths, is a huge win. It’s the kind of design we don’t really see anymore, as most modern games lean more toward PvP-focused elements.

I just wanted to replay my very first [b]Resident Evil[/b] game and relive how it felt - and it honestly holds up better than I remembered. The atmosphere still feels tense, while action is constantly present and keeps things engaging. One thing I also appreciated is how the game brings together so many of the series’ most iconic characters under one title. Seeing Leon, Chris, Ada, and others all involved in the same overall conflict gives the story this “big crossover” feeling, slowly revealing how everything fits together and answering questions as the narrative unfolds.

I’m planning to replay the entries I’ve already finished and also experience the [b]Resident Evil[/b] games I missed, in order to better understand the full lore and piece together its complex story—with all its iconic characters and tangled factions!
The game is amazing, but it lacks on smooth animation

Despite a few settings that could be modified, the game looks better than I expected. I don't understand why other players don't like this game. Maybe I'm biased because I love action, but so far, awesome resident evil game I ever played!

I really wanted to like RE6 bros. It's one of the longest in the series, has four full campaigns that actually connect, and has more actual horror than RE5. On paper this should have been the best one.
Instead the PC port is a glitchy ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ mess. Even with mods and DX11 patches it's still broken as ♥♥♥♥. The final boss in Leon's campaign is literally unplayable — the lightning rod stabbing prompt never shows up no matter how close you get, how many times you reinstall, or what controller you use. I can't even finish the game because of this one bug.
What the ♥♥♥♥ were they thinking? Massive potential completely wasted. With a real patch this could have been kino.

The most joy you will get from this is when you uninstall it.
Hands down one of the worst games I have ever played. 
Do not waste your money or, more importantly, your time; go replay Resi 4, or even Resi 5. Resi 6 is such a step down from earlier instalments, it probably shattered every bone south of its pelvis.... which would explain the shoddy character movement. 
If you're even slightly curious about the shambling mess that game calls its "plot"; go watch it on youtube, or better yet, just read the short paragraph or two on the wiki - it'll be faster, and less painful.

My first Resident Evil game that I bought and finished. When I played it on the switch mid 2023, I instantly fell in love with the series. This game inspired me to get Resident Evil 5, which I also adore.

i got the game as a birthday gift from my boyfriend and i love it !!! it does crash sometimes but it doesnt disturb the gameplay. it usually crashes during agent hunt or right after cutscenes. re 5 and 6 are way too overhated, they are actually my favourite games from the franchise. i definetly recommend, especially with a friend!!

This game has things it does good and bad, I liked the action and the cutscenes/story, but I didn't really like the gameplay. 90% of guns use a open crosshair with a red dot that constantly moves through it which is soo much more annoying than if the entire camera just swayed more. The four campagns feel unique though which is good because they all have a different feel to them, Leon with the slightly more spooky gameplay, not scary because its really still action, chris feels even more action, I don't know how to describe Jakes campain but I do like his character, and ada has the more stealth focused game. Overall I enjoyed it, just not more than any other game in the series.

Under almost no circumstances should you ever play this game. Whether you’re a new RE fan or if you’re just curious about this game for whatever reason, it’s best to just read the cliff notes version of this game’s story and move on to something else. Even if you have a friend willing to play any coop game with you, just don’t. It’s not even serviceable as an experience so bad that it’s funny. As a matter of fact, this likely is the only game in the history of the medium that is somehow not elevated by the simple inclusion of coop.

I unfortunately dragged a poor soul to play this game with me thinking the game might be at least fun as a coop experience. Boy, was I wrong.

There are 4 different campaigns in this game, with each having returning characters being accompanied by a new face. Leon is accompanied by Helena, Chris with Piers, Sherry with Jake, and Ada with a figment of her imagination. They all intersect with each other’s storylines at certain points, which is fine. Ambitious even. But playing each campaign individually just feels like a slogfest, where most chapters just feel like they drag on needlessly and overstay their welcome. Often times, the gameplay got interrupted every 10 seconds (no really, sometimes the game took us off a cutscene and then put us back into a new one as soon we take 5 steps forward) just to show a pointless cutscene, like maybe the camera pointing at an obvious path. The characters themselves just often make questionable choices, feel out of character sometimes (Leon in most of his campaign), or just have a moment of awkwardness. For example, [spoiler] there is a moment in Sherry’s campaign where she gets rescued by Ada Wong just as Sherry was facing certain death and gets reunited with Jake. Ada Wong swings away like SpiderMan. Both Sherry and Jake proceed to never acknowledge that ever happening and simply continue the story without even saying so much as a simple “thank you” or even questioning anything that just happened. It’s like nothing ever happened. [/spoiler]

As for the regular gameplay, this game marks the first time in the mainline series where you can aim and move. Unfortunately, that’s where the positives end. Shooting at enemies sometimes does not feel responsive whatsoever. About 75% of the enemies just transform after killing them the first time, in which case they either gain a very specific weakness or become bullet sponges. Nonetheless, it just becomes a chore to clear out an area at times. As for QTEs, there is nothing else I can say about the QTEs that hasn’t been said in other reviews. Like what the hell kinda combination is R + Space? What do you mean I only have a femtosecond to press that combination? All I can say is just don’t take your hands off your controller/keyboard if you’re in a cutscene. There are also moments in the campaign (particularly Chris’s second half of the campaign and most of Ada’s) where it is apparent that they were running dry in their budget so they start slowing down the rate of cutscenes shown or just reusing assets/scenes freely. 
(Speaking of Ada’s campaign, it is obvious that it was meant to be a single player experience but then Capcom remembered halfway through developing her campaign that the rest of the game is actually co-op and so they just added some nameless, faceless goon that is somehow able to teleport himself to wherever Ada’s position is. Doesn’t even appear in cutscenes too.)

While all those gameplay features make for some ♥♥♥♥ experiences, the biggest offender by far has to be the escape sequences. In every campaign, there is at least one escape sequence designed with the sole purpose of giving the player an aneurysm. Sometimes the camera angles changes by itself which screws over the direction your character is going, leading to your death. Or there is an obstacle that you’re barely able to see that completely stops your entire momentum, leading to your death. Or the collision with your co-op partner causes you to slow down significantly, leading to your death. Or… you get the point. And they leave little room for mistakes so get ready to restart at least once if it’s a lengthy sequence. Easily the worst escape sequence is in Chris’s campaign when you’re in a harbor and you must run on various platforms nonstop or you get blown up. The game decides to give you such limited space to run that you’ll be constantly bumping into your co-op partner. In addition, if you graze the invisible wall, there’ll be one single polygon of obstruction protruding out from that wall that causes you to stop running entirely. But that’s not all, if you hold the run button/key, you’ll likely mount a ledge… and then dismount that same ledge immediately after. Death. I know that’s only one segment, but that segment singehandedly left such a bitter taste in our mouths that we just felt completely miserable even after getting past that part. 

It should also be noted that if you still do intend to play the game because you’re a masochist, then there are a few bugs that can affect the flow of the game. For example, your cursor can disappear in the inventory menu, making it difficult to operate it. Another example, in Sherry’s campaign (maybe chapter 3 or 4?), Jake has to release the bars to a door so Sherry can proceed into an alley. For whatever reason, this causes Jake to become catatonic when you get him to interact with another prompt, soft locking your game right there. What worked for us was resetting the whole game and giving it another try. If that doesn’t work, try switching characters and give that a try. Alternatively, you can just stop playing the game entirely and play/do something else actually worth your time. Actually, just do that anyways. Watching the cutscenes on Youtube will save your sanity. I mean, this game almost killed off the entire franchise for a reason.

I haven’t even gone more in depth with the other problems this game has but you’ll have to trust me on this when I say avoid this game at all costs. Never in my life have I ever felt so excited to get a game off my SSD.

In short
The good:
1. Good graphics. Still hold up decently.
2. Ambitious concept for a story structure
3. You can uninstall it
4. It was so ♥♥♥♥ that Capcom got their heads out of their ass and rebooted the series with far better RE sequels, with 7,8 and 9 being considered all time classics already 
The bad: 
1. Everything else
feeling a little extra grateful for RE7 because this was one of the worst games I have played. It was a  bore to play and a struggle to finish. makes RE5 look like a masterpiece

Bueno, este es el sexto título de la saga y lo jugué justo después de pasarme los 5 anteriores y, de todo corazón, digo: ¡es el peor de todos!

Primero, aclaremos la parte técnica. Los controles son aceptables a veces; jugar con teclado y ratón no se siente tan raro como en Resident Evil 5, pero aun así se nota muchísimo que está pensado para jugarse con control. Hay acciones que en PC no tienen mucho sentido y otras veces terminas haciendo movimientos que no querías porque hay demasiadas acciones para muy pocos botones.

La cámara también juega muchísimo en tu contra. Hay momentos donde cambia entre cámara en tercera persona y ángulos fijos como si fuera Resident Evil, y recuerdo más de una ocasión donde tenía que huir pero la cámara se acomodaba justo de forma que no podía ver hacia dónde correr. Literalmente, la cámara termina siendo el verdadero enemigo y eso se vuelve frustrante muy rápido.

Y ni hablar de que aquí no existe la pausa ni siquiera en las cinemáticas. Hay momentos donde el juego te quita el control en una escena, pero los enemigos siguen golpeándote y cuando termina la cinemática ya estás muerto. Horrible diseño.

En cuanto a jugabilidad, siento que esto es un Call of Duty con mods de Resident Evil. En juegos anteriores, aunque los enemigos fueran absurdos, al menos la historia intentaba darles algún sentido. Aquí parece que los diseñadores de enemigos y los escritores nunca hablaron entre ellos y luego intentaron juntar todo a última hora. Hay partes donde parece un juego de Indiana Jones y otras donde  salen enemigos que parecen zombies sacados de Skyrim.

Lo peor es que demasiados enemigos usan armas, al punto de que a veces se te olvida que estás peleando contra infectados y no contra soldados normales. Te sientes en cualquier otro shooter genérico. Incluso las coberturas están mal puestas y rara vez ayudan.

Al menos agradezco que esta vez el compañero sea inmortal, porque si no, honestamente no creo que hubiera tenido la paciencia para terminarlo.

Sobre la historia… hubo momentos donde simplemente dejó de importarme. Tiene 4 campañas conectadas entre sí y al principio sí daba curiosidad jugar la siguiente para ver cómo se relacionaban, pero al final sentí que muchísimo era relleno. Y como dije antes, cuesta tomarse en serio la historia cuando los enemigos y las situaciones son tan absurdas. Sé que la historia nunca ha sido el punto fuerte de Resident Evil, pero aquí me pareció de las peores de la saga.

La verdad, no lo recomiendo. Si quieres conocer la historia, mejor mira un resumen en YouTube; ni siquiera siento que valga la pena ver todas las cinemáticas o un gameplay completo. Solo jugué una campaña con un amigo y diré que puede ser divertido en cooperativo… pero únicamente si no has jugado otros shooters mejores y lo único que conoces de la saga es Resident Evil 4.

I've tried defendi8ng this game.
But is COmplete fuciking ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥.
the ai teamate constantly gets you killed. Doesnt follow the command promptsd.

and top it all off.
the FUCXKING cutscenes dont pause thre game. so there is a chance of you getting hit immediately after it ends and there is NOTHING you can ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ do about it.

By far the worst mainline resident evil game I've tried. I am trying to play it to complete everything, but I'm really struggling to enjoy it. Everything feels terrifically bland with the worst controls and camera imaginable. A possible 2 minutes were spent configuring the controls by capcom for PC.  I do not understand how they could release this turd between RE5 and RE revelations 2. Both of which, even with their faults, blow this game out of the water. It just lacks the special sauce needed to be a good RE game.

I can't believe I'm typing this: but this is even worse than RE 8 village. It would be one thing if this was a decent action third person shooter, but it clearly isn't. I could forgive it and even value it if it had tighter controls with all its hoards of zombies to plow through. But as it is, just run this game to get the story out of the way.

this game is extremely overhated sure it's a long way from the normal resident evils but for the game itself it's pretty mindless fun

Piers open the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ door that's an order!

Gears of Resident Duty: Sleeping Dogs Edition. Complete with ridiculous scenarios, repeat boss fights, horrible pacing, and the worst UI setup in any video game ever created. Oh and a buggy mess that makes it almost impossible to properly finish without major issues.
this game is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ ass i cant believe it didnt indefinitely kill the franchise forever. me and my bro are gonna finish it though because we are masochists i guess

Undeniably [b]one of the worst main Resident Evil games.[/b]
buuut.. It's kinda [b]so damn bad it's kinda good[/b]. 

I'm a pretty big RE fan. 
I've [b]LOVED[/b] every main entry after RE6. RE7 onwards is absolute peak for me and I hold these games close and dear to my heart. 
When RE6 came out and even to this day for many fans and players was a very low point for the franchise. (So they later revived with the amazing RE7 >:) buut back to RE6)

I did play RE6 with a friend but we only got to play a bigger chunk of the Leon campaign as my game wouldn't stop crashing later in the EP and would always restart from a far away point. Plus among the crashes there were some softlocks within the plane which also required us to restart.

The only 2 redeeming qualities about this game is it's ok action and absurd comedy.
It takes itself very seriously, but is wholly unserious and incredibly ridiculous and cheesy. 
If you're playing with a friend and are in mood for an absurd action game and have a beer or two, this might have you laughing a lot. 

I won't go into detail here as it would be too long, but there's just so so so so soo unbeliably much tha's so off and funny and ridiculous about this game xD 
ONE very funny example I will give is: It always only takes 1 zombie to completely blow up or derail the vehicle the main characters are driving/riding in xdd
Play or watch it and you'll probably find out very quickly for yourself.

Me and my friend were taking it non-seriously as that's the only way most people can enjoy it, and we had a reeeeeelatively fun-ish time, shaking our heads, laughing and seeing the incredible unbelievable unfolding madness and cheesy lines and story.

I'm glad I captured it for us :D I was dying of laughter editing the footage.
and If I want a good laugh again I will revisit the footage ^^

RE5 was a bit more serious, believable and better made. we finished that one, and even though we were also laughing how ridiculous that one was from the start (for example zombies falling like slipping on a banana animation xd) 
It was still less cheesy and ridiculous than RE6

I gave RE5 a 3/5 which was being somewhat generous.
I can't give RE6 anything more than a...

My rating: [b]Absolutely Ridiculous 2/5[/b]

I played this game many times. First in xbox 360, second in xbox one and now on steam😅

It's an underrated entry that deserves more credit than it gets. While it moves away from traditional survival horror and leans heavily into action, it makes sense within the series’ story, showing how BOW's evolved into a global threat. 

The multiple campaigns were a great idea, offering different perspectives that connect into one larger narrative.
The gameplay is fast, intense, and packed with cinematic moments, and the character roster makes the experience even stronger. It may not deliver the same fear and tension as classic entries, but as an action-focused chapter in the Resident Evil saga, it is exciting, ambitious, and highly enjoyable. A bold and entertaining game that many fans judged too harshly.

I hope there will be a standalone for Jake and Sherry in the future.🤘🏻

I enjoyed it so much! I don't really get where's the hate coming from. I had so much fun playing this game, even on singleplayer :) Yes, it's denifitely different from other RE games, there's a lot of action and well absurd sequences, but to me that was part of it's charm. I believe there's many people that only tried Leon's campaign which, in my opinion, is the most boring one. I still had fun, but that story was the least interesting one, that's for sure. Personally, I didn't find Helena particularly appealing character, she was okay but not a memorable one. I didn't really fall in love with RE6 until I started Chris' campaign. That was a great story, but my absolute favorite is Jake's and Sherry's one. I'd love to see them together more, I adore these two <3 Capcom please let Jake out of your prison
The only thing I really can't stand: the controls here are really annoying, take some time getting used to. Maybe it's easier on pad. Also: the worst parts of all the campaigns are the ones where you have to drive some crappy vehicle or flee. Fighting with bosses? No problem. A car chase? HORRIBLE

the events of the game and characters make for a solid story so it is a fairly fun game so my overall rating is a 6\10, my detailed review is as folow:
Gameplay: 3\10 (most of the crucial moments in the gameplay are fixed cameras wich completely ruins the controls order, secondely the controls are quite confusing because sometimes to escape it's space+R or R or right\left mouseclick or sometimes it's the movement buttons, very very confusing)
Characters, Environement and the variety of enemies and gameplay: 7\10
Story and Lore: 2\10 (whitout reading the files), 7\10 (with the files)
Graphics and details: 5\10
Refunded cuz of poor performance and bought it on PlayStation, overall the game is peak and one of my fav re games, Ada’s campaign is awful tho

I enjoyed RE6 a whole lot more now than I did on release. The game has four different campaigns, each with their own tone, but with one overarching narrative.

I played the chapters in this order: https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=937551032. It's a good mix of chronological order, while also ensuring story beats that would recontextualize the past are right where they should be.

I think the game's mortal sin was encouraging players to play Leon's campaign first, with his first two chapters being some of the longest in the game. On release, there were no mid-chapter checkpoints. You had to beat the chapter start-to-finish in one sitting, otherwise you had to start over. 

It's a little rough around the edges, but still a fun time. Especially with a friend.
There is a mission on Jakes story that requires both jake and sherry to unbolt doors and I cannot progress past this no matter what. :( fix the game
if you are Resident evil fan you like this but two things i dont like in this in this game,camera in this game is so bad not good , and movment in this game so high so speedy
Pretty fun if you ignore the glitches and almost game-breaking bugs

good game, good action, goood tale, but NOT good control settings. Its more for any gamepad. Anyway, i dont play any RE since march 2026. Now, after already complete remake 2,3,4, biohazard and village, re6 is a good for better understanding logic every part of series.

I do not get the hate. Like this game is really any different from any other RE game nowadays.

Super duper fun co-op... like please play with a friend
Extremely fun combat, probably the best in the whole franchise. 
Campaign is really good and diverse in its gameplay scenarios, such as driving a motorcycle or flying a jet.
Also hands down the best Mercenaries mode out of any of the games especially the No Mercy mode, which will have you fighting off double the amount of enemies, makes it feel like a real apocalypse with the droves and droves of enemies. It really makes you think of how to use the environment and how to clear out hordes efficiently while keeping up your time and score.

Though I will it admit it kinda does feel like a generic cover shooter of the 2010's at times. But seriously the gameplay sets it apart with the unique loadouts every character gets and the hand to hand combat and finishers will make you feel like a total badass, along with all the Dark Souls rolling and sliding ♥♥♥♥ you can do as well. 

Though I can completely understand the stigma around the game, just grab a friend and give it a go.

8/10

Back at 2013, Resident Evil 6 fits perfectly for a handful plate, with its abundant content and outstanding cutscenes and UI design. I am not sure whether RE6 is the first RE that provides split-screen coop and online coop function, but it works smoothly during the playthrough by me and my partner, leave alone the adjustment of screen resolution quite anti-instinct. 

Four major campaigns is introduced to the game and a series of different game modes to experience, and the collection of documents from each playable character features a unique display. Player could feel the genuine craftsmanship by the designer, which is a solid thumbs-up for the game.

Though not as mesmerizing as Resident Evil Remake, RE6 occupies a unique place in the franchise, especially with its chapter happened in Hong Kong, where Cantonese broadcast and menu are just everywhere.

Somehow despite being all over the place sometimes, the game still manages to be a worthwhile experience. The story is definitely over the top, but I like how to plot comes together once you play all the campaigns. Some clunk here and there, but it's manageable. Not a 10/10 for sure but still fun for what it is.

bad resident evil game, but a good action game. it's fun as hell if you play it with a friend (which i did). the 4 POVs are a fun change and i grew attached to the new characters, especially jake. this game doesnt deserve all the hate when re5 is by far worse imo
This game deserves to be re-evaluated. Of course, I don't think it is a well-made game, but I would rate it as the best in the series in terms of action.
Mecánicas innecesarias, Bosses sin explicación, Enemigos recontra estresantes, pero recontra disfrutable en Multijugador y ofrece una variedad de tramas para mantenerte ocupado.

This is the most fun I've ever had playing a videogame. 

The gameplay and related mechanics were enjoyable. You have a variety of dodge buttons, plus the option to simply sprint to dodge. Handling the inventory space is not confusing, though not a tetris game either. I do like tetris, but for a game intended to be played with a partner, the simple two-layer (one for weapons/heals & one for hand-held explosives) interface they designed just made sense. They really prioritized speed and accessablity, and it shows. Quick-time events the game pushes on you were difficult when you weren't expecting them, but did not break immersion or cause further frustration and instead added a funny challenge. There were also twin prompts that needed both players, which I'm glad were added. I need to know which one of the main characters is the macho and which one gets to be pillow princessed up and around. That Piers guy was glad too, I'm sure. Driving in this game, like in many games, just required for you to get used to the controls and it was smooth sailing from there. The boss fights were typical boss fights, where you had to observe attack patterns and weakspots to get to the gist of it. The only exception to that was Leon's final boss. Still don't know how I beat that guy.

For the best playthrough experience, this should definitely be played with a partner. I enjoyed this game immensely because I got to play with a friend who was as invested as I was in icons like Leon, Ada and Chris, but also ready to explore new faces like Piers Nivans and Helena Harper. The way the stories overlapped and certain cutscenes were repeated were always a point of compelling discussion and we both loved putting our on spin on the way our characters interacted between cutscenes. Not only was our friendship a catalyst for the best possible playthrough ever, we grew even closer because of it. Your enjoyment of this game might rely purely on who you're playing with.

The way the fixed camera angles were positioned during a few escape scenes made little sense and erred off the flow of the story. Namely these 2 times: 
In Chris's campaign at the very end, while running away from the final boss up a spiraling platform. Suddenly having to switch for forward W to backward S because the developers decided this would be a cool shot of the boss (which I'm only assuming happened) got me killed more times than I can count.
In Jake's campaign Jake and Sherry have to run away from Ustanak in a straight line with the camera positioned in front of them, facing them and the monster behind them. That scene ends with them having to climb a ladder that was out of the frame because of the perspective. Or would end, if you first found that ladder.
But this is really not a big deal. 8/10 times everything controlled nicely or it was just a matter of endurance.

It's.. peculiar, that despite this being a co-op game, the last campaign was clearly designed to be played alone. In Ada's campaign the extra agent does not show up in cutscenes at all, cannot interact with any doors or puzzles, and does not have to be present when opening up doors leading to loading screens as the secondary protagonist (unlike in every other campaign). He's like Ada's imaginary friend. I played him, and had lots of fun messing around while my friend playing Ada was trying to puzzle the ♥♥♥♥ out of everything. Still, peculiar...

This is not the most cinematic game out there. The graphics are good, but not on the same level as the engine Capcom uses today. The lighting and rendering of the characters look visually very pleasing. It's rather the environments that sometimes feel flat. Overall we didn't encounter any bugs, though the camera freaks out a little if you try looking up while sprinting.

This game was so fun that we had to set a time limit for each session. After playing a game for too long jokes don't flow that easily and we quieted down, and that's not how I wanted to play this game! It was always a pleasure to open up resi6 and get immersed in the story, culture and characters.

not a bad game mercs are fun leons story is good. chris's story.  is okay  jakes is eh.
 adas is whatever.

Outside of the less-than-sub-par porting regarding controls and resolution and whatnot, this is one of the best, upmost THRILLING action games I have had the pleasure of experiencing. There's so much more to the combat system than so many players bother looking into, it's honestly quite sad it's not looked into and taken inspiration from in other games. Learning it is fun, and playing the game itself is an absolute blast (even moreso when combined with a co-op partner! in which you absolutely should play with one, almost a necessary factor) with plenty of campaign content that has thoroughly upheld my attention through all three and a half campaigns. This is an action movie disguised as a game in the best ways possible, and I wouldn't have it ANY other way.

Notice how I didn't mention Resident Evil anywhere in that.

Simply STOP looking at this game like a Resident Evil installment, and enjoy it for what it is. You want horror? To feel dread and eerie suspense? Then learn from all the other various reviews who tell you that this isn't a horror game, and save your money instead of review bombing an over ten year old game, because this is the furthest thing from an RE game and shouldn't be rated as such. Otherwise, grab this game when it's on a discount, and have a grand ol' time quickshot, blowing up, shooting, sliding, diving, and parrying around.

Been playing it with my friend. I really love the game. The gameplay is fun, the feature is fun and o course the story. I hope they make this kind of game again, when you play it in couch with your friend or just play it online. Once again I love it!

This is the most mixed I've ever felt on a game because genuinely it's some of the biggest ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ I've ever had the misfortune of playing but at the same time some of it is so ridiculous that I just can't stop having fun. Every pointless explosion or some character being thrown around like a ragdoll is genuinely such effortless comedy, especially when you can tell this game wants you to take it seriously soooo badly, which is a problem. This game takes itself too seriously, with the exception of Ada's campaign, which embraces it's camp and is definitely one of the two good campaigns in the game [the other being Jake and Sherry's], though even then that's kinda stretching it considering the sometimes miserable gameplay choices just make it a massive chore.

The best part was definitely the co-op. I played the entire game from start to finish with a friend and it was one of the best co-op experiences I've had in a video game, which is saying something considering the game made me want to tear my hair out and then make soup out of it. If you have a friend to play with and are looking for something dumb to do, I seriously recommend you pick up this game with the expectation that it'll be the dumbest, most annoying and yet endearing experience you would ever get from a Resident Evil game.

[b] If you're going in expecting a good game, I'd look somewhere else. Unless you have a friend to bring along. Then maybe consider getting this on a giant sale. [/b]

[b] [url=https://backloggd.com/u/hisuianpitch/review/4701689/] 2/5. Longer review with spoilers here. [/url] [/b]

I think it's a bad game in the series. Especially playing the same places over and over again with 4 characters, except for a few spots, got boring. I think the storytelling is not that good and is bad, but unfortunately, you have to play it to understand the future of the characters.

Okay, I wish there was a "Mixed" review option for this one. I've played it multiplayer, and multiplayer with someone you know it's a lot of fun even if you WILL curse at a few of the more unfair deaths. I was the dedicated sidekick player and it went great, except for the bit where player 2 sometimes gets sidelined against final bosses (Not spoiling anything in case you're somehow interested in playing the game without knowing what happens in it but--the one final boss fight where the sidekick does NOT get sidelined almost makes me forgive the sidelining in the others).

The writing is wildly inconsistent, and Leon especially suffers from characterization issues that'll give you a headache.

Singleplayer, meanwhile, I've heard enough horror stories to make me never wanna try. 

I can vouch that it's a good game multiplayer, but ONLY get it if you have someone to play it with, or you'll hate it.

Shot my friend with a rocket launcher and got my friend and I killed. 10/10

Totally fun with a friend! How the UI changes with each character is a lot of fun too. It's got so many twists and turns in this story. Tears will and have been shed from this game, but I still highly recommend it. It aged very well! Play with your duo because if you play this game with the right person, you will find yourselves laughing at all the dumb stuff you can do to each other.

A lot of RE players dislike this game, but for me its good, cuz we can play with friend, i love RE series but i never play it myself, cuz im scared lol, im always watch the gameplay/playthrough on yt, so this game is good for me.

Overall 5/10
This game has an identity crisis, it doesn´t know if it wants to be horror, edgy, action, cringe or serious. It mixes together all of this but doesn´t flesh out anything in particular. To start with the positives, Id´s say the control scheme and overall gameplay are genuinely so strong it´s crazy. Gameplay and combat is smooth, fast paced and fun, paired with the epic dodges  and rolling on the ground. Art direction is also a very strong point , just looks good. Shame it´s so dark, would be nice to see anything. The separated campaigns are an interesting approach, Sherry´s one is the best, definitely.
Her chemistry with Jake is easily the best part, paired with the story overall. Another shame is that the main villain, Nemesis from wish, is kinda undercooked, understandable with the campaign split. It would be much better if the entirety of RE6 would be just Sherry´s story. 
The positives are there, the game can be fun (definitely coop), but the negatives outweight the good stuff.
The inventory in this game makes sense with the gameplay, but it also robs the game of any kind of pause button. Skillpoint system is undercooked, bossfights are nothing to write home about (Leon´ms final boss), story is just a mess all around, lack of weapon upgrades, and the most important negative, you can trip on a zombie.
The biggest issue is the identitty crisis, and the worst part is the story. Leon´s story is generic "zombie action movie", you take Leon out and it falls flat. Chris´s is a mess, action all around without a breath to take, in a bad way, the story is the best part, it was very good and emotional, but the final bossfight took years off of my life. Sherry´s is the best, and Ada was supposed to tie it all together, which it did so partially. BUT, Ada had the best partner, THE legendary goat.
Overall, the cons slightly outweight the pros, but I had fun at the end. As I made clear, the most replayable part is Sherry.

only just started, will update as i complete each campaign, initial thoughts are

wow this controls so much better than re5 did, it feels significantly more enjoyable to play, however! whatever they were thinking making it so convoluted to use herbs or even pick out your secondary items i have no clue. had to look up a guide multiple times so far just to heal myself with both herbs and a first aid spray

also feels like theyve put effort into creating some sort of survival horror feel, where the last game kind of was lacking that

the ui feels crazy? why am i looking at some sort of experimental playstation menu for my inventory, and why do i have google maps ar glasses to help me navigate the environment

ive never played left 4 dead but some of these shootout sections feel like what i imagine that game series to be

definitely still leans pretty heavy into action, though id say there feels like a little more of a balance here compared to the previous game, at least so far - maybe when i start the redfield campaign ill feel different

finished leons campaign! thoughts time
- whoever said each chapter is only like an hour long lied bro these took me 1h30 - 2h each, i was probably exploring a lot more than i needed to but come on pal
- why was the prologue just a direct copy of a sequence in chapter 5 
- suffers a little from the problems of re5 where the game occasionally has an expected way for you to do things and deviating from it will just eat all your ammo
- giant underground platforming cave annoyed me
- pretty good overall! much better than i was expecting
Mediocre combat, but good story and fun. Overhated.
One of the best third person games that I've ever played, and this is coming from a diehard OG Resident Evil fan.
if you're coming for a resident evil game its pretty bad. The mercenaries tho? supa hot fire

I have nothing but utter hatred for this game. Every time I come back to play it on a different platform I keep thinking it wasn't as bad I remembered it but it somehow just elevates itself further and further. Does not help that this is a Capcom port in the era of them being absolutely horrendous at them AND with me having an UHD monitor which does not like this game at all.

Gameplay wise? Yikes. This is by far the worst experience you can have in the mainline of Resident Evil. There are so many instances of just losing control of your character and it taking far too long for you get it back. Leon's campaign is riddled with these little stumbling set pieces. Having the camera get ripped out of your control to look at something you don't need to know about because it's a linear ass game with a button you can press to see where to go. Only for you to realize that the enemies can move and will hit right as you get out of the cutscene. Usually meaning an instant down on higher difficulties. Speaking of getting down. What's the point of having a Last Stand mechanic if it takes you 8 to 10 seconds for it to finally let you use your gun while their enemies are around you. I also can not in enough words express how tired I am of transforming enemies AND enemies with guns. This game ramps that up to 11 in some of the later chapters.

The UI in this game. Holy hell. RE5 with it's plethora of weapons was fine because you just chose which weapons you wanted. You'd have 2 types on Chris and 2 types on Sheva. Then you'd have room for ammo, which stacked high, and didn't have certain ammo types dropping. This game? Let's give you ALL the weapons AND the ammo has dinky stack counts. Constantly having to sit there and futz around with the awful UI to drop things to pick up a box of ammo to just immediately reload the gun I actually want to use is just poor planning. There's no reason why the ammo stack count should be so low or the inventory being so limited.

The campaigns drag on and the overall story is just some dude who had Yellow Fever and was transforming women into an Asian woman who then got pissed and decided to destroy the world. Just nonsense. Spread along 4 campaigns where, for most of them, not much really happens. Not to mention Ada's campaign is a questionable tacked on addition where only her first mission has any true added relevancy to the plot and they couldn't even properly do a story where she would have a co-op partner.

If you're desperate for a co-op game and somehow haven't played this game yet BUT you've already played RE5 then sure get it at 5 dollars. Just temper your expectations and you might have an okay time while you question the game and the decisions they've made along with your buddy.

Oh, and if you have an Ultrawide then get Flawless Widescreen. It'll *sort of* fix the issues with the ugly UI.
I wish this game was more fun. I recently gave it a good to see if it was as bad as I remember and yeah it's not good
What an awful game, bad story, bad writing and dumb gameplay

The game gets a lot of flack because it dove into action more than horror. But it's really not that bad of a game. It tried to be a jack of all trades. Offering something for anyone who wanted to play it. A zombie story with Leon, an action heavy with Chris, and something akin to RE3 with Jake. But it fails to do any of these greatly. It's not a bad game. Just a product of when it was made.

Honestly how did/do ppl hate on this game when they love RE4 or even RE9?
Play the game with the mindset of a character action game (DMC, Bayonetta, etc) rather then strictly a horror game. Use the melee! Get close and personal! Dodge/slide around! You will not have nearly as much fun if you do not engage with the game mechanics and only shoot.

this was an unexpected fun time. the story is nuts and insane and there is almost no horror to be seen. but its really enjoyable and still feels resident evil. its just a natural progression from five. also to speak of progression. In re5 you still had the you had to stand still while shooting. this movement system is off the hook. the mechanics are vast and the devs took their time applying different styles to different characters. even the hud and everything. almost nothing was copy and paste. my only real complaint is it can be a lil janky sometimes. and adas section feels a lil underwhelming with agent. but canonically i understand why it couldnt really involve teamwork like the other ones but still. other than that fun ass game with da homies
This game ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ sucks. Just watch a lets play. Please avoid this game at all costs

I really enjoy the coup of this game, together with a friend. but the PC port is medicore and the story not so good. 

I still like it though

the game is fun to play i really enjoyed the cool combat style and gunplay and fighting same for the extra gamemodes there not so bad the story was a little much to understand but it was still cool and all the chrachters have the unique fighting style but the only thing that sucks about this game was there are some parts where the camera angles are wacky and can you can fail alot or there are some parts where they are buggy so id recommed watching guides but overall the game is super fun to check out
This is one of the best games ive ever played and my favorite Resident Evil. co op is awesome!

All style and no substance. A line said by a character in the game that many would agree suit this game quite well. I however, don't quite agree.
Don't get me wrong. There are quite a few problems with this game, but I don't think they're quite as severe as most people make them out to be.

This biggest obstacles that I had to get over was the atrocious UI and quick time events. The way that you use hotkeys to swap around weapons and items is very unintuitive as first, but after about an hour or two it becomes second nature and really wasn't that bad at all anymore.

 The second thing was the quick-time events.. I never like these in most games, and I sure as hell don't like them here. The best part about them is that if you don't like them, you can turn them off! There are a few mandatory scripted moments in the game where they are mandatory, but all in all aren't bad.

 The third and final gripe was the bosses. They are hit or miss. Some were exciting, somewhat challenging and fun to play out, and others were incredibly infuriating. They can all be beaten easily enough with a retry or two, but I think some of them could have really used a little more time in the oven.

As far as performance, I ran the game with a DX11 patch and framerate unlocker, and other than about three instances where a character got stuck on an obstacle, a few random frame drips and the uber hyper sped up vehicles due to the game being tied to the engine, the game was smooth almost all the way through. However, there is a boss section during Chris' chapter (3-2) that crashed the game for me every time unless I changed the game to windowed mode and/or drop the framerate to 60 or below. I just did both to make sure. 
I almost forgot, if you set the framerate higher than 60 FPS, there is a specific insectoid type enemy that moves INCREDIBLY fast. The enemy is already anger fuel, and doesn't appear too often, but it's something to keep in mind.

 After all that is said and done, the game really isn't that bad. It has beautifully played out cutscenes, good voice acting, fun gunplay, a few interesting stealth sections and tense (albeit a bit too frequent) action packed moments. It's one of the weaker Resident Evil games, and has almost nonexistent horror,  but in my opinion, it's not outright bad.
 
If you're a fan of the faster paced Resident Evil game ala RE5 with a darker tone, and are hungry for a bit more of the same, then look no further.. Just don't expect a masterpiece.

honestly this game is clunky and a bit of a drag, but it's worth if if you're a big RE fan and wanna play co-op with someone. the characters are interesting, even if the gameplay is more actiony than all other RE games lol.

this is not as fun and polished as RE5 is- there are 4 campaigns that all basically rehash the same story from different povs with mostly the same cutscenes (though there are some differing major boss fights). but i became a big fan of the jake/sherry and chris/piers campaigns. it was wonderful to see an adult sherry in addition to three new characters, all of whom have interesting personalities/backgrounds. (helena is a new character also). i think the stories and character development for some of these characters were actually pretty good, but the package it was delivered in was tedious to open. that by the time i got to the good stuff i was over it lol. 

this is a game that would have been better off as a movie, but as a piers fan, i'm happy that it exists but sad that this game is considered the reason RE fell off as a series. when i recommend this game to others, i just preface it with "this game is tedious but worth it if you like the characters". definitely get it on a sale because i think most people wouldn't think it's worth it at full price. and it's much more fun to play co-op than solo.
Crappy UI, story’s all over the place, boss fights aren’t all that great, Piers is dead and the game gave me motion sickness. I’ll give this game a generous 6/10.

I just finished playing Resident Evil 6 with my friend, and honestly, I really enjoyed it. The storyline was amazing. it felt like I was playing a game and watching a movie at the same time. Playing on Veteran difficulty was really hard, but playing co-op with a friend made the experience so much better and more enjoyable. One thing I really loved was how the game had multiple campaigns and showed connected events from the perspectives of different characters. Seeing how all the stories matched together was really convincing and made the game feel unique and exciting. I honestly wish they would make more Resident Evil games like this. It was a really fun experience from start to finish.

This game was some of the most fun I had with friends recently from playing the regular campaigns to just messing around in the extra modes! I liked the fact that the story was split between so many characters and that there were multiple campaigns. I enjoyed being able to figure out the story with each campaign! It definitely feels different coming from the remakes but it is soo fun in its own right. I wish we could wear the costumes in the normal campaigns though.
This game is terrible, but in a so bad it's good way, not a unplayable mess.

Just another game in Resident Evil's identity crisis era before Resident Evil 7.

This is barely even a Resident Evil game and more like just a generic zombie shooter game with a generic plot, and even then you have much better options if you just want to shoot zombies. Also there are more quick time events than actual gameplay.
Most funniest Resident evil game , Not worth if ur looking for some horror game this is action packed third person game , Black Sheep of resident evil Good for CO-OP fun

the game is amazing

Fun gameplay, but some campaigns really need to chill. Everybody dies in Leon's and its annoying, for example. But the gameplay is fun so long as you're doing coop, Jake is really cool and I wish he was his own character and not attached to Wesker in any way. Still, fun game. Bad story.

If you want a good RE game. This is not it. RE5 was the precursor ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. That was also not it.

This is a badly written game. On top of a mostly bad co-op game. RE7 was made to correct the wrongs of this game. RE6 is like a b-tier movie that is not so bad its good. Its so bad its bad. 

Also you will run into bugs from 13 years ago. That if you want to complete co-op you have to look into. Its stupid.

A game with potential that's let down in large part by camera jank and imprecise controls.

It's a shame because you can tell that significant effort went into making impressive set pieces and some creative combat scenarios here & there as part of an over the top action-movie-sequence type game. They even put in a few special executions of staggered enemies that catch you off guard in a good way.

But it is all heavily let-down by a schizophrenic camera lacking in smoothness which almost single-handedly ruins the combat flow. Other camera related problems contribute such as a quite excessive shaking effect from time to time and sometimes even control being ripped away from the player in parts where an indicator to draw attention would have sufficed.

There are flashes of brilliance in the combat but awkward player character movement (some strange mix of tank movement and semi-omnidirectional movement) and the ease with which the cover shooter mechanics either get activated when not desired (or the opposite) really brings it down a fair bit.

The game could have become a classic like RE4 if the camera and controls had been tightened up considerably.

The game also has plenty of QTEs which I don't particularly care for either.
Old game.  Chris's harbor level is soo strict that it will kill your run.  Just wait until Capcom re-makes this game or just read the lore.

This game is an absolute trainwreck, its game mechanics are simultaneously too much and too little, its character are stereotypes that I've seen a million times, the bosses are utterly nonsensical, and it is an INSULT to the Resident Evil Franchise.

10/10

Don't let anyone tell you this game isn't garbage; it is

I went into this after reading many reviews saying that it was underrated and got undeserved hate, I guarantee you that's not the case. Shooting a gun never felt so awkward in any game I've ever played, somehow melee feels weird, even running looks awful. 
What really did for me was the way healing works, using herbs give you like tictacs or something and you have to press the heal button the correspondent number of health points you lost to gain them back, it's just feels and looks silly.

This game have the worst control and camera angle I have ever seen in video game history. Chase scene are obnoxious with force camera angel whenever you proceed to scripted event. PC control feel bad to play too. And the laser accuracy is just feel bad to play. Story is cliche, not entertaining. Too many plot armor for the main pair and also for the main plot too. So many opportunities to prevent the rocket from launching, but always slip away so the city get demolished anyway. Will only go through this one time and never again touch this garbage.
Meh. Too many overlapping story plots with little actual effect on one another. Can be quite confusing to follow, and I don't even remember what the story is about. Long ah game.
